_README: Statement-wave assignments for v2 rows authored after X1 reconciliation. build_v2_manifest.py merges these rows into the manifest, assigns phase/repo/formal_name/source-verification metadata, and drives the statement=done overlay entries.

X2.implemented_by: Codex X2 clean-11 statement authoring pass
X2.source_verified_by: Codex X2 clean-11 source readback pass

axv_1610_00876_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 3 as existence of the least minimum-semidegree Mader bound for subdivisions of TT_k.

axv_1610_00876_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 4: every oriented tree has some minimum-outdegree bound forcing a subdivision.

axv_1610_00876_02.note: Encodes Conjecture 7: delta-plus-maderian digraphs are closed under disjoint union.

axv_1702_01607_00.note: Encodes Problem 3 with the recursively defined S_i tournament family and directed domination number.

axv_1702_01607_01.note: Encodes Problem 4: large tournament domination forces a bounded-order subtournament of large domination.

axv_1812_02420_03.note: Encodes Problem 5.40 as existence of an arc relation on b-subsets whose induced acyclicity exactly matches nonempty common intersection.

axv_2305_15585_00.note: Encodes Question 10 using a same-carrier simple graph relation over the tournament vertices and degeneracy in an out-neighbourhood.

axv_2305_15585_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 11 as the cycle-containing out-neighbourhood special case.

axv_2409_05039_00.note: Encodes the split-digraph strong 2-kernel question, including the no-source hypothesis and |K| <= |G|/2 bound.

axv_2503_23191_00.note: Encodes Question 5.1: semidegree at least k/2 forces every non-antidirected orientation of the k-edge path.

axv_2602_16333_02.note: Encodes Question 4.3: connected vertex-transitive digraphs have pairwise-intersecting longest directed cycles.

X2.faithfulness_verified_by: x2-faithfulness-verify workflow wf_cf217e26 (11 independent second readers) + adversarial re-verification of the corrected split-digraph row wf_33ede959, 2026-07-08
X2.faithfulness_result: 10/11 confirmed faithful on first pass. split_digraph_strong_two_kernel_statement was found PROVABLY FALSE (a directed triangle with self-loops satisfied the hypotheses but had no strong 2-kernel); the object must be ORIENTED. Fixed by adding an explicit x2_oriented guard (loopless + digon-free); adversarially re-verified (machine-checked counterexample exclusion + exhaustive oriented-split-digraph search to n=12, zero counterexamples; axiom-free positive witness). All 11 now faithful and genuinely open.

X3.implemented_by: Codex X3 clean-13 statement authoring pass
X3.source_verified_by: Ptolemy independent X3 faithfulness readback pass

axv_1509_06563_00.note: Encodes the bounded-clique generalisation: for nu>0 and k>=3, large chi and omega<k force holes of nu consecutive lengths.

axv_1509_06563_01.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.5 using the recovered context definition of k-constricting/constricting sets.

axv_1702_01094_00.note: Encodes the rainbow-hole question: bounded omega and large chi force, in every proper colouring, a hole with s consecutive rainbow vertices.

axv_1702_01094_01.note: Encodes the stable-cover question via a finite family of stable vertex subsets covering G and an s-vertex induced path uniquely covered at each path vertex.

axv_1705_04609_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.8: sufficiently large chi forces either a kappa-clique or holes of ell consecutive lengths.

axv_1910_00697_00.note: Encodes the solved Esperet problem as existence of an induced-hereditary chi-bounded graph class not bounded by any natural-coefficient polynomial in omega.

axv_2110_00278_00.note: Encodes Conjecture 1.3: for every forbidden forest H, H-induced-free graphs satisfy chi(G) <= omega(G)^c for some c>0.

axv_2201_08204_00.note: Encodes Question 3.1 as arbitrarily large chromatic graphs with omega=3 whose triangle-free induced subgraphs all have chromatic number at most 3.

erdos_1111.note: Encodes Erdos Problems #1111: bounded clique number plus large chi forces disjoint anticomplete vertex sets with large subset chromatic numbers.

std_galvin_r_dl_conjecture.note: Encodes the Galvin-Rodl conjecture: large chi and omega<=k force an induced subgraph with chi>=r and omega=2.

std_gy_rf_s_complementation_conjecture.note: Encodes the Gyarfas complementation conjecture: complements of classes whose induced subgraphs satisfy chi<=omega+c are chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_conjecture_6_8_h_h_h_1_class.note: Encodes Gyarfas Conjecture 6.8: the class satisfying alpha(H) omega(H) >= |V(H)|-1 for every induced subgraph H is chi-bounded.

std_gy_rf_s_sumner_conjecture.note: Encodes the two-forbidden-graphs Gyarfas-Sumner statement using absolute bounded chromatic number, complete graphs, and forests.

X3.faithfulness_verified_by: Ptolemy independent X3 faithfulness readback pass, 2026-07-08 + independent adversarial cross-check wf_4d0ecbcb (13 readers, 2026-07-08)
X3.faithfulness_result: 13/13 confirmed faithful. No well-formed-but-unfaithful issues found in statements or local primitives. Residual assumptions recorded by reviewer: 'colouring' in the rainbow-hole row is proper colouring; natural-coefficient polynomials/natural exponents are faithful discretizations of polynomial bounds on integer graph invariants; finite indexed stable covers are no loss for finite graphs. The Scott-Seymour tournament out-neighbourhood row was explicitly deferred as a directed/tournament followup, not part of the undirected X3 batch. | Independent cross-check (distinct reader set): 13/13 faithful, quantifier-order trap cleared on every chi-bounded row.
